course_title: Project Management and Commercialisation in the Pharmaceutical Industry
course_description:
Students taking this course will gain insight into the ethics and business-related aspects of product development in the pharmaceutical industry (MTP Sector). Aspects important in the product development life cycle that will be addressed include: history, role and purpose of ethics including the cultural safety in, and social impact of, research in society; legal responsibilities in research and the need for intellectual property protection; various commercialisation pathways that can bring a product to market; the business of product development touching on principles of project and financial management. Students will meet career professionals in these roles in the pharmaceutical industry and learn about the real-life practical application of theory learned.

Welcome to the course profile for PHIP7162, a course that forms part of the Masters of Pharmaceutical Industry Practice (MPIP) Program. Please email or see the course coordinator during/after lectures or tutorials for any clarification related to the learning objectives and assessment. The lecturers and course coordinator look forward to your active participation in PHIP7162.
timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
All timetable information will be detailed in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for this course.
course_aims: Students will develop an understanding of the importance of intellectual property (IP), its role in the patent process and commercialisation in the pharmaceutical industry. Students will be introduced to the essential role that ethics plays in the course of product development in Australia, addressing the requirements as set out in the National Statement. They will learn the key principles of project and financial management.

learning_outcomes.description: Recognise the roles and diverse perspectives of human research ethics committees
Describe the purpose of the human research ethics committee and the National Statement
In a collaborative/group work setting, apply different intellectual property (IP) protection strategies, evaluate their impact on a commercial model and know how patent law applies to an invention
Evaluate the various commercialisation pathways of a novel product/invention and demonstrate the pros and cons of each
Describe and apply the principles of project management, identifying possible risks that may occur and reasons for project success
Identify common stakeholder roles and responsibilities in project management
Identify the different categories of expenses in product development

assessment_summary.title: Intellectual Property and Patent Law
assessment_summary.due_date: 21/08/2025 3:00 pm; Complete the quiz within the 24 hours prior to this deadline.

assessment_details.title: Intellectual Property and Patent Law
assessment_details.due_date: 21/08/2025 3:00 pm Complete the quiz within the 24 hours prior to this deadline.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment is a 'general knowledge' quiz in the form of multiple choice questions, and short answer questions related to Intellectual Property (IP) and Patent Law content covered in Module 1 in class activities (+ UQ Extend 'Module 1'). This is an individual assessment that you are required to undertake without any form of communication with other students. This quiz is open book, so you may refer to any resource while completing it. This is an online quiz undertaken on your own device and in the venue of your choice. The quiz will comprise 15 questions selected randomly from a pool of questions, meaning each student will have different questions from their peers. In the case of MCQs there is only one correct answer from five options listed. You will have 50 minutes to complete the quiz. ONE attempt is permitted. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Access the quiz at the link on the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .

assessment_details.title: Commercialisation Pitch
assessment_details.task_description: Students will work in groups (4 max.) to evaluate different commercialisation pathways by addressing the pros and cons of each, based on Module 2 in-class activities (+ UQ Extend 'Module 2'). You will then need to agree (as a group) on which pathway would succeed in a given case and explain the reasoning via a recorded ‘group pitch’ to a virtual investor. Detailed instructions on preparing your group pitch recording will be provided in Week 1, during the course introduction lecture. All members of the group must participate. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for the marking criteria for this assessment. You will also be required to submit a completed Group Charter, at the same time. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your recorded group pitch (all members to participate) via the submission point on Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
You can request an extension on behalf of your group for a group assessment item, provided that at least 50% of your group members also agree to the request for an extension. Download and complete this form, and attach this form to your extension request:
Extension of Group Assessment - Group Member Acknowledgement (PDF, 144.9 KB)
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Human Research Ethics Application
assessment_details.task_description: In this individual assessment, based on Module 3 in-class activities (+ UQ Extend, 'Module 3'), you will be provided a full clinical trial protocol to read and analyse. You will be required to ascertain relevant information necessary to complete question categories outlined in a provided Human Research Ethics Application (HREA) template. Further detailed requirements and marking criteria are available on the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit responses to the HREA-related questions in the template provided via the link in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.

assessment_details.title: Project Management plan - Gantt chart
assessment_details.task_description: In this individual assessment, based on Module 4 in-class activities (+ UQ Extend, 'Module 4'), students will be asked to draft a project plan. This will involve preparing a Gantt chart of a small, discrete project showing the project timeline addressing the principles of project management, including scope, any risks, and how they can be mitigated. See the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for the template and marking criteria. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your completed Gantt chart template at the link in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: UQ Extend Module 1 - IP, Patent Law Self-directed learning: In your own time, review and make notes on UQ Extend Module 1 content, PRIOR to the in-class activities covered in Week 2 & 3. Learning outcomes: L03

UQ Extend Module 2 - Commercialisation Self-directed learning: In your own time, review and make notes on UQ Extend Module 2 content, PRIOR to the in-class activities covered in Week 4 & 5. Learning outcomes: L04

UQ Extend Module 3 – Human Research Ethics Self-directed learning: In your own time, review and make notes on UQ Extend Module 3 content, PRIOR to the in-class activities covered in Weeks 6 to 9. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

UQ Extend Module 4 - Project Management and Financial Management Self-directed learning: In your own time, review and make notes on UQ Extend Module 4 content, PRIOR to the in-class activities covered in Weeks 10 to 12. Learning outcomes: L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 5
learning_activities.topic: IP, Patent Law & Commercialisation Learn the importance of Patent Law and the different commercialisation pathways that can be employed in product development as well as understand the concept of Intellectual Property. Content delivery is via lectures and tutorials, with an in-class time of 4 hours per week. Learning outcomes: L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 9
learning_activities.topic: Ethics in Human Research and Product Development Ethics is a critical, integral aspect of human research. Learn the importance and role of ethics in medical product development. Content delivery is via lectures and tutorials, with an in-class time of 4 hours per week. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 12
learning_activities.topic: Project Management and Financial Management Learn the key aspects and considerations of Project & Financial Management. Learn how to make a basic Gantt chart for a project, in a tutorial setting. Learn about the different categories of costs and expenses to be considered when generating a research protocol budget. Content delivery is via lectures and tutorials, with an in-class time of 4 hours per week. Learning outcomes: L05, L06, L07
